task_completion.description: A blank canvas is opened and visible in an image editing application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image editor (like GIMP) is open, displaying a blank (typically white) canvas workspace.

task_completion.description: The paintbrush tool is selected to draw on the canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The paintbrush icon is highlighted or actively selected in the application's tool palette or toolbar.

task_completion.description: At least 3 distinct brush strokes are created on the canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: There are 3 or more separate, disconnected continuous lines or marks drawn on the canvas.

task_completion.description: The brush strokes cover a noticeable portion of the canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The strokes are thick or long enough to cover at least ~5% of the canvas area, rather than just being tiny, barely visible dots.

task_completion.description: The brush strokes have good contrast against the background.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The strokes are drawn in a color that clearly stands out against the blank canvas (e.g., dark strokes on a white background).

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to draw the strokes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with the canvas and toolbars in the image editor, rather than terminal commands generating an image.

integrity.description: The strokes appear to be manually drawn using a brush tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: The marks on the canvas have organic, hand-drawn paths or brush textures, rather than perfectly straight geometric lines, pasted images, or text.

integrity.description: The agent drew on the provided blank canvas rather than opening a completed file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression of screenshots shows the canvas starting blank and strokes being added sequentially.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task involves using GIMP. The original canvas is blank white (800x600px). To pass, the final image must have at least 3 distinct stroke regions, at least 5% canvas coverage, well-distributed strokes, and good contrast (average contrast > 30).
